title: Kokera acuminata

However, I can provide information on a different plant, Kokera (Piper kudrinowii) or alternatively, Kokera (Piper kudrinowii) is also known as 'Piper kudrinowii' or 'Kokera', a plant in the Piperaceae family, native to India. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

The bioactive constituents of Piper kudrinowii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
